home.noSentenceFallback: Your sentence details have still not been confirmed by NDCS. Contact Records for more information.

almostEligible.title: Become Eligible For Good Time Restoration
almostEligible.body_one:
{{count, number}} more month with no Misconduct Reports and you will be eligible to request a good time restoration.
If you become eligible, staff will automatically initiate a request and inform you of the decision.

You currently have {{goodTimeLostDaysRestorable, number}} total days lost.
almostEligible.body_other:
{{count, number}} more months with no Misconduct Reports and you will be eligible to request a good time restoration.
If you become eligible, staff will automatically initiate a request and inform you of the decision.

You currently have {{goodTimeLostDaysRestorable, number}} total days lost.

ineligibleLTRH.title: Become Eligible For Good Time Restoration
ineligibleLTRH.body:
Despite no disqualifying recent Misconduct Reports, facility staff have noted you are currently ineligible for good time restoration due to your current or recent placement in Long-Term Restrictive Housing. Please speak with facility staff for more detail.

You currently have {{goodTimeLostDaysRestorable}} total days lost.

ineligibleTreatment.title: Become Eligible For Good Time Restoration
ineligibleTreatment.body:
Despite no disqualifying recent Misconduct Reports, facility staff have noted you are currently ineligible for good time restoration due to your refusal of recommended clinical programming. Please speak with facility staff for more detail.

You currently have {{goodTimeLostDaysRestorable}} total days lost.

reentryChecklist.title: Complete Your Roadmap to Reentry
reentryChecklist.body: Complete the items on your reentry checklist to prepare for success.

reentryAssessment.title: Complete 120 Day Reentry Assessment
reentryAssessment.body: Your Reentry Specialist has assigned this assessment to you so they can better understand your needs before your 120 day meeting with them. This will enable them to bring more customized resources and clear answers to your meeting.

trd.label: Tentative Release Date (TRD)
trd.summary: Your Tentative Release Date (TRD) is the estimated date you will be released from incarceration. This date can be changed as a result of a disciplinary action, a change in good time, a parole revocation, or a change in the law or the interpretation of the law.

ped.label: Parole Eligibility Date (PED)
ped.summary: The Parole Eligibility Date (PED) is the date you are first eligible for parole.

mmtd.label: Mandatory Minimum Term Date (MMTD)
mmtd.summary: Your Mandatory Minimum Term Date (MMTD) is the date when the required minimum part of your sentence ends. The mandatory minimum term is set by the law based on your total combined sentence.

gbmd.label: Good Time Balance/Mandatory Discharge (GB/MD)
gbmd.summary: A person sentenced to prison immediately gets this time up front. This is a type of good time that can be removed for Misconduct Reports (MRs).

lgtr.label: Lost Good Time (Restorable)
lgtr.summary_zero: You do not have any lost good time days that are restorable.
lgtr.summary_other: This good time has been removed due to misconduct reports (MRs). You may apply to have these days restored after some time without misconduct reports. Ask your case manager for specific details on when you are eligible to ask for time back.

lgtn.label: Lost Good Time (Non-Restorable)
lgtn.summary_zero: You do not have any lost good time days that are not restorable.
lgtn.summary_other:
This good time has been lost due to misconduct reports (MRs). It is not possible to restore these days.

This is already applied to your Tentative Release Date (TRD).

lb191.label: LB 191 Earned Time Credits
lb191.summary: After the first 12 months, you may earn an extra 3 days off your max sentence each month for good conduct. These days are applied to your Tentative Release Date (TRD) when they are earned.
lb191.summaryOtherLaw: You were sentenced under LB {{lawNumber}}, not LB 191. For more information, submit your questions to the records office personnel in your facility on an Inmate Interview Request (IIR).

jailCredits.label: Jail Credits
jailCredits.summary: Jail credits are the amount of days the court determined you spent in jail for the criminal charge which led to your prison sentence. These days are subtracted from your total combined sentence.

confirmed.default: NDCS staff confirmed this as complete for you.
confirmed.state-id-license: NDCS staff confirmed some form of ID. However, the ID may be expired. Please check with staff for more.

unconfirmed.default: NDCS staff have not yet confirmed this as done. Speak with your case manager.

reentryChecklist.subtitle: Mark tasks as complete to keep your case manager updated on your progress

unsavedChangesModal.title: Unsaved Changes
unsavedChangesModal.message: You have unsaved changes to your Roadmap to Reentry. Are you sure you want to discard them?

early.title: Complete Anytime
early.subtitle: These tasks can be completed as early as possible to set you up for success.

3-years.title: Complete 3 Years Pre-Release
3-years.subtitle: Career readiness should be prioritized especially within 3 years of release.

6-months.title: Complete 6 Months Pre-Release
6-months.subtitle: Start your housing and employment plan within 6 months of release.

120-days.title: Complete 120 Days Pre-Release
120-days.subtitle: Finalize your reentry plan. Your Reentry Specialist can assist with these items at your 120-Day meeting.

items.birth-certificate: I have my birth certificate, or have applied to receive one.
items.ged-diploma: I have my GED or high school diploma, or am enrolled in GED classes currently.
items.job-training: I am taking part in job training, industries, or an apprenticeship to prepare me for a career.
items.skills-career-interests: I know my skills and career interests.
items.parenting-programs: *If I have a family,* I am participating in parenting or family programs.
items.five-keys-interest: I know which of the 5 Keys programs I am interested in participating in.
items.career-programs: I am enrolled in college, vocational, or training programs to prepare me for a career.
items.mental-health-assessment: I have completed a mental health or substance use assessment.
items.parenting-programs-2yr: *If I have a family,* I am participating in parenting or family programs.
items.clinical-program: *If relevant,* I have completed or am on track to complete any prescribed clinical program.
items.job-opportunities: I know what kind of job opportunities I can apply for.
items.resume-portfolio: I have a resume or work portfolio.
items.safe-housing: I have a safe place to live after release.
items.backup-housing: I have a backup housing plan.
items.reentry-circle: I know when my Reentry Inner Circle Meeting is, or I have already attended.
items.social-security-card: I have my Social Security card.
items.state-id-license: I have my state ID or driver's license.
items.medical-checkup: I have completed a medical check-up.
items.continuation-care-plan: *If relevant,* I have a medical continuation-of-care plan (appointments, providers, or referrals).
items.medication-supply: *If relevant,* I will leave with a 30-day supply of medications plus refills.
items.treatment-aftercare: *If relevant,* I know where I can go for treatment or aftercare if needed.
items.barriers-plan: I have a plan to deal with driver's license or employment barriers.
items.budget-plan: I have a budget plan for my first month after release.
items.legal-obligations: *If relevant,* I know my legal or financial obligations (child support, fines, debt).
items.parole-expectations: *If relevant,* I understand what to expect while on parole.
items.supportive-person: I have at least one supportive person or mentor I can count on.
items.peer-support: I am connected to peer support or reentry programs.
items.success-plan: I have a Success Plan with goals for 2 weeks, 30, 60, and 90 days.
items.community-help: I know where to go in the community for extra help.
items.transportation-plan: I have a transportation plan for release day and for work/supervision.

mmtd.heading: Mandatory Minimum Term Date (MMTD)
mmtd.body:
Your Mandatory Minimum Term Date (MMTD) is the date when the required minimum part of your sentence ends. The mandatory minimum term is set by the law based on your total combined sentence.

If your sentence has a mandatory minimum, you must serve all of that time before good time is applied or earned.

<PolicyCallout text="For more information, research

- **Nebraska Revised Statute 28-105**
- **Nebraska Revised Statute 83-1,107**

in the Law Library.

Alternatively, submit your questions to the records office personnel in your facility on an Inmate Interview Request (IIR)." />

ped.heading: Parole Eligibility Date (PED)
ped.body:
The Parole Eligibility Date (PED) is the date you are first eligible for parole. The Nebraska Board of Parole sets Parole Hearing Dates based on the outcomes of reviews. At the hearing, the Nebraska Board of Parole will make a decision on whether to grant parole, deny parole, or defer the decision to a later date.

Your PED depends on your sentence terms and any changes to your good time.

A mandatory minimum sentence is the minimum time you must serve before becoming eligible for parole. Your Parole Eligibility Date can never be before the mandatory minimum sentence date.

You are eligible for parole upon the earliest date of the following:

- (a) “When the person has served one-half the minimum term of his or her sentence as provided in Nebraska Revised Statute §§ 83-1,107 and 83-1,108”;
- (b) “If serving max term of 20 years or less, two years prior to the person’s mandatory discharge date” (Tentative Release Date); or
- (c) “If serving a max term of more than 20 years, when the person has served eighty percent of the time until the person’s mandatory discharge date” (Tentative Release Date)

If your parole eligibility date or tentative release date is changed, you will be given written notice of the new tentative release date or parole eligibility date.

Changes related to parole eligibility dates from LB 50 have already been reflected in dates.

<PolicyCallout text="For more information, research
**Nebraska Revised Statute 83-1,110**
in the Law Library.

Alternatively, submit your questions to the records office personnel in your facility on an Inmate Interview Request (IIR)."
/>

trd.heading: Tentative Release Date (TRD)
trd.body:
Your Tentative Release Date (TRD) is the estimated date you will be released from incarceration. This date can be changed as a result of a disciplinary action, a change in good time, a parole revocation, or a change in the law or the interpretation of the law.

If your parole eligibility date or tentative release date is changed, you will be given written notice of the new tentative release date or parole eligibility date.

<PolicyCallout text="For more information, research

- **Nebraska Revised Statute 28-105**
- **Nebraska Revised Statute 83-1,107**

  in the Law Library.

  Alternatively, submit your questions to the records office personnel in your facility on an Inmate Interview Request (IIR)." />

gbmd.heading: Good Time Balance/Mandatory Discharge (GB/MD)
gbmd.body:
## What it is

The GB/MD (Good Time Balance and Mandatory Discharge) days are issued up front. This is reflected in your TRD (Tentative Release Date) upon intake.

## How it works

If you don’t have a Mandatory Minimum sentence, you receive six months for each year of your term and pro rata (proportion) for any part of your term which is less than a year.

If you have a Mandatory Minimum sentence, your GB/MD days will reflect good time based on your Mandatory Minimum Term Date (MMTD).

## Losing Good Time: Restorable / Non-Restorable

This good time can be removed for Misconduct Reports (MRs).

## How to Get Time Back

If you lost restorable time, you can earn it back by following the rules. **NDCS staff will now automatically start this process** for you within one month of you becoming eligible.

## Can I Get My Time Back?

This is a summary of the policy to **restore lost good time**. For the official legal rules and information on how to appeal a decision, please refer to **NDCS Policy 117.02** in the Law Library. LB 191 and other forms of Good Time are different, and have separate policies.

To be eligible to restore lost good time, you must meet these rules:

- **No guilty finding of "Class I" Misconduct** for the last 1 year
- **No "IDC" Misconduct** for the last 6 months.
- **No more than 2 "UDC" Misconducts** for the last 6 months.
  - **Note:** These time limits start from the date of your last misconduct report.
- **Stay out of Long Term Restrictive Housing:** You cannot be in LTRH now, and cannot have been there in the last 90 days.
- **Participate in Treatment:** You must accept and go to all clinical programs recommended for you.
- **Time Left:** Your tentative release date (TRD) must be more than 4 months away.

## How the Process Works

1. **Staff Starts the Request:** Once you meet the rules, a staff member will put in the request for you.
2. **The Decision:** The Warden will approve or deny the request within 20 days.
3. **Getting More Time:** Initially, you can get 30 days back at a time, once a month.

   - If you get 30 days back every month for 5 months in a row and you still have some lost good time left, you can then start getting 60 days back each month.
   - After three months of 60-day restorations, you may be eligible for 90 days back each month.

4. **If You Get a New Misconduct Report:** If you stop meeting the rules, the process resets. You will have to go back to earning 30 days at a time for 5 months before you can get more again.
5. **If Denied:** If your request is turned down, staff will wait 90 days before trying again.

lb191.heading: LB 191 Credits
lb191.body:
LB 191 credits give you 3 extra days off your Tentative Release Date (TRD) each month for good behavior. These days are applied to your Tentative Release Date (TRD) when they are earned.

If you don’t have a mandatory minimum sentence, you can start earning these days after your first 12 months of incarceration without any disqualifying class one, class two, or more than three class three misconduct reports. There’s no limit to how many credits you may build up, and once you have earned them, they cannot be taken away.

If you do have a Mandatory Minimum sentence, you can start earning these credits after serving the mandatory minimum term without any disqualifying misconduct reports.

<PolicyCallout text="For even more information, research
**Nebraska Revised Statute 83-1,107**
in the Law Library.

Alternatively, submit your questions to the records office personnel in your facility on an Inmate Interview Request (IIR)." />

jailCredits.heading: Jail Credits
jailCredits.body:
Jail credits are the days the court determined you spent in jail for the criminal charge which led to your prison sentence. These days are subtracted from your total combined sentence.

<PolicyCallout text="For even more information, research
**Nebraska Revised Statute 83-1,106**
in the Law Library.

Alternatively, submit your questions to the records office personnel in your facility on an Inmate Interview Request (IIR)."
/>
